seoTitle: No 6 Branch vs Nobby — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between No 6 Branch and Nobby. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: No 6 Branch vs Nobby: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in No 6 Branch and Nobby.
heading: No 6 Branch vs Nobby — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between No 6 Branch, Queensland and Nobby, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: No 6 Branch has a lower overall climate risk score (27/100) compared to Nobby (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is No 6 Branch or Nobby safer from flooding?
faq.answer: No 6 Branch has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Nobby scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: No 6 Branch scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Nobby scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in No 6 Branch or Nobby?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. No 6 Branch has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
